title: Daily Summary — 2026-04-29
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window: 2026-04-28T12:11:30Z to 2026-04-29T12:11:30Z. Signals show 18 issues, 10 PRs, 1 discussion, and 0 commits updated.

Activity centered on orchestrator UX, add-rubrics routing, GitHub-hosted runner stability, access/grant UX, and new project-management style automation ideas.

## Recently Active PRs

- [#20 Make orchestrator explicit via /orchestrate and label route](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20) — Open with `CHANGES_REQUESTED`; reviews flagged behavior/design gaps around explicit orchestration.
- [#15 Add minimal add-rubrics route with dedicated workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15) — Open; latest review still calls for fixes around `agent/rubrics` write concurrency, commit/push failure reporting, and prompt schema consistency.
- [#21 Fix GitHub-hosted runner stability issues](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/21) — Open; discussion narrowed follow-up to keeping the direct runner fix while handling `.agent/dist` cleanup without breaking downstream runtime steps.
- [#7 Add auto-running label control for orchestrator handoffs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/7) — Open; review requested cleanup of duplicated heuristic gating and stop-path label cleanup coverage.
- Merged in the synced window: [#6](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/6), [#11](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/11), [#12](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/12), [#13](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/13), [#16](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/16), [#18](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/18).

## Recently Active Issues

- [#33 orchestrate longer more complex tasks](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/33) — New planning discussion proposed a tracking-issue orchestrator above the single-target orchestrator.
- [#31 UX: design an explicit grant for external users to use the agent](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/31) — Answer proposed one-shot maintainer grants instead of widening global access policy.
- [#30 More flexible branching strategy](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/30) and [#29 Allow point by by feedback](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/29) — Newly opened.
- [#28 Add including `dist (and node modules etc)` for the installation skills](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/28) — Answer linked the issue to runner/install stability work and scoped safer docs/skill/.gitignore changes.
- [#26 Automatically report issues on GitHub](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/26) and [#27 Automatically check sepo agent updates](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/27) — Triaged as create-action requests; #26 has an open decision between Discussions vs Issues as central intake.
- Other synced active issues include [#5](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/5), [#8](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/8), [#9](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/9), [#14](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/14), [#19](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/19), [#22](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/22), [#23](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/23), [#24](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/24), [#25](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/25), and [#34](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/34).

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#32 Daily Summary — 2026-04-29](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/32) — Daily summary discussion was present in the synced signals; no comments were recorded.

## Follow-ups

1. Fix or decide next action for open PRs [#20](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20), [#15](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/15), [#21](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/21), and [#7](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/7).
2. Confirm whether failed-run intake for [#26](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/26) should use GitHub Discussions or Issues.
3. Triage newly opened strategy/UX issues [#29](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/29), [#30](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/30), [#31](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/31), [#33](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/33), and [#34](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/34) into implementation plans or tracking epics.
